ORDER DENYING CROSS-DEFENDANT VICTOR MARQUEZ'S SPECIAL ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE

LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT VICTOR MARQUEZ'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ATTEMPTED EXTORTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY ELIZABETH SOUSA IS DENIED. THE MERITS OF THE MOTION WILL BE ADDRESSED EVEN THOUGH THE MOTION WAS FILED OUTSIDE THE 60-DAY TIME PERIOD OF CCP 425.16(F). MR. MARQUEZ RECEIVED AN EXTENSION OF TIME TO FILE HIS RESPONSIVE PLEADING AND REASONABLY BELIEVED THAT HE COULD STILL FILE AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. MR. MARQUEZ'S SEPTEMBER 11, 2017 LETTER CONSTITUTES ATTEMPTED CRIMINAL EXTORTION AS A MATTER OF LAW AND THUS THE LETTER DOES NOT CONSTITUTE PROTECTED ACTIVITY PER CCP 425.16. THE LETTER STATES THAT ASIAN, INC. IS WILLING TO FULFILL ALL PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS IF MS. SOUSA TURNED OVER THE ART AND PROVIDED AN ACCOUNTING. THE LETTER FURTHER STATES THAT: "IF MS. SOUSA DOES NOT AGREE TO THESE CONDITIONS, WE WILL HAVE NO CHOICE BUT TO REPORT HER TO THE CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE AND THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE FOR THE CHARGE OF LARCENY." THE LETTER THREATENS TO REPORT MS. SOUSA TO THE AUTHORITIES UNLESS SHE RELINQUISHES THE ARTWORK. IT IS THEREFORE ATTEMPTED EXTORTION. (STENEHJEM V. SAREEN (2014) 226 CAL.APP.4TH 1405, 1417-1418.) MR. MARQUEZ MAKES MUCH OF MS. SOUSA'S MISCITATION OF THE SEPTEMBER 27, 2017 E-MAIL AND HIS SUBSEQUENT COMMUNICATIONS THAT PURPORTEDLY SHOW THAT HE HAD NO INTENT TO THREATEN MS. SOUSA. THE E-MAIL DID NOT CLEARLY UNEQUIVOCALLY RESCIND THE PRIOR THREAT. MOREOVER, MR. MARQUEZ DOES NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY HOLDING THAT HIS SUBSEQUENT COMMUNICATIONS COULD SOMEHOW "WIPE THE SLATE CLEAN." MS. SOUSA'S REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED AS THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE WAS NOT FRIVOLOUS. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-15-2018 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Continued To Dec-04-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Cross Defendant Victor Marquezs Special Anti Slapp Motion And Special Anti Slapp Motion To Strike Defendant And Cross Complainant Elizabeth P Sousas First Amended Cross Complaint.
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Victor Marquez, Esq. of Marquez Law Group, 415.848.8971, appearing as self-represented litigant/Cross-Defendant;
Nicole Meredith, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, 415.398.0200, appearing by CourtCall for the Cross-Defendant Asian, Inc.;
Alan Korn, Esq. 510.548.7300, appearing for the Defendant/Cross-Complainant Elizabeth Sousa.

Cross-Defendant Victor Marquez's Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Defendant And Cross- Complainant Elizabeth P. Sousa's First Amended Cross-Complaint is ARGUED.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Cross-defendant Victor Marquez's anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike the third cause of action for attempted extortion in the first amended cross-complaint filed by Elizabeth Sousa is denied. The merits of the motion will be addressed even though the motion was filed outside the 60-day time period of CCP 425.16(f). Mr. Marquez received an extension of time to file his responsive pleading and reasonably believed that he could still file an anti-SLAPP motion. Mr. Marquez's September 11, 2017 letter constitutes attempted criminal extortion as a matter of law and thus the letter does not constitute protected activity per CCP 425.16. The letter states that Asian, Inc. is willing to fulfill all payment obligations if Ms. Sousa turned over the art and provided an accounting. The letter further states that: "If Ms. Sousa does not agree to these conditions, we will have no choice but to report her to the City Attorney's office and the District Attorney's office for the charge of larceny." The letter threatens to report Ms. Sousa to the authorities unless she relinquishes the artwork. It is therefore attempted extortion. (Stenehjem v. Sareen (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1405, 1417-1418.) Mr. Marquez makes much of Ms. Sousa's miscitation of the September 27, 2017 e-mail and his subsequent communications that purportedly show that he had no intent to threaten Ms. Sousa. The e-mail did not clearly unequivocally rescind the prior threat. Moreover, Mr. Marquez does not cite any authority holding that his subsequent communications could somehow "wipe the slate clean." Ms. Sousa's request for fees is denied as the anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike was not frivolous. Order signed. (see order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Victor Marquez, Esq. of Marquez Law Group, 415.848.8971, appearing as self-represented litigant/Cross-Defendant;
Nicole Meredith, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, 415.398.0200, appearing by CourtCall for the Cross-Defendant Asian, Inc.;
Alan Korn, Esq. 510.548.7300, appearing for the Defendant/Cross-Complainant Elizabeth Sousa.

CROSS-DEFENDANT VICTOR MARQUEZ' DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S ELISABETH P. SOUSA'S 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is ARGUED.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Cross-defendant Victor Marquez's demurrer to the third cause of action for attempted extortion in the first amended cross-complaint filed by Elizabeth Souza is overruled. Liberally reading the cross-complaint as required by CCP 452, it adequately alleges sufficient ultimate facts to infer Mr. Marquez's specific intent to commit the crime of attempted extortion. As explained in the ruling on Mr. Marquez's anti-SLAPP motion to strike, Ms. Sousa pleads a claim for attempted extortion. Order signed. (see signed order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Cross Defendant Victor Marquez Cross Defendant Victor M Marquezs Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.5 And 128.7 Against Defendant And Cross Complainant Elizabeth P Sousa
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Victor Marquez, Esq. of Marquez Law Group, 415.848.8971, appearing as self-represented litigant/Cross-Defendant;
Nicole Meredith, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, 415.398.0200, appearing by CourtCall for the Cross-Defendant Asian, Inc.;
Alan Korn, Esq. 510.548.7300, appearing for the Defendant/Cross-Complainant Elizabeth Sousa.

Cross-Defendant Victor Marquez's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.5 And 128.7 Against Defendant And Cross-Complainant Elizabeth P. Sousa is ARGUED.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted: Cross-defendant Victor Marquez's motion for sanctions is denied. Mr. Marquez fails to show that the third cause of action for attempted extortion in the first amended cross-complaint filed by Elizabeth Sousa is frivolous or solely intended to cause delay. Order signed. (see order for full details).
